reason: Response B has the terse, declarative, short-sentence cadence characteristic of Haiku—plain phrasing ("Tomorrow will be hard, and that's okay") and economical emotional logic without flourish. Response A is smoother, more polished, and leans on warmer stock motivational phrasing ("You've got this," "facing this head-on") that reads like a larger model imitating an upbeat comforting register. The clipped, slightly flatter affect and structural simplicity of B fits Haiku more than A's elevated, well-rounded prose.
